description: QuantChat - Wire WebSocket for LIVE messaging: add missing API routes for conversations, connect the existing RealtimeProvider/useRealtimeChat to the conversation page so messages are sent via WS and displayed in real-time, add typing indicators and presence to the UI, add read receipt sending on message view.
steps: Step 1: Create a shared proxy helper at apps/quantchat/src/app/api/_lib/proxy.ts following the pattern in apps/quantmail/src/app/api/_lib/proxy.ts but using env QUANTCHAT_BACKEND_URL (default http://localhost:3002).

Step 2: Add missing API routes: (a) apps/quantchat/src/app/api/conversations/route.ts - GET (list conversations) + POST (create conversation), (b) apps/quantchat/src/app/api/conversations/[id]/route.ts - GET single conversation, (c) apps/quantchat/src/app/api/conversations/[id]/messages/route.ts - GET messages + POST send message. Each uses the new proxy helper.

Step 3: Update apps/quantchat/src/services/api-client.ts to set baseUrl to '/api' (relative, targeting Next.js API routes) instead of 'https://chat.quant.app/api'. The getConversations() should call /api/conversations, getMessages() should call /api/conversations/{id}/messages, sendMessage() should call /api/conversations/{id}/messages POST.

Step 4: In apps/quantchat/src/app/chat/[id]/page.tsx, ensure the useSendMessage hook also calls sendRealtimeMessage from useRealtimeChat so the message is broadcast to other participants via WebSocket in addition to the REST POST. Merge incoming WS messages with REST data (already partially done - verify deduplication logic works).

Step 5: Wire typing indicators: In ChatInput, call setTyping(true) on keypress with a 3-second debounce, and setTyping(false) on blur/send. The TypingIndicator component already renders from typingUsers array - verify it displays properly.

Step 6: Wire read receipts: When the chat page mounts or new messages arrive from other users, call markRead(messageId) for the latest message. Display read receipt status on sent messages (the ChatBubble component accepts a status prop).

Step 7: Add an online/offline presence indicator in the chat header. The useRealtimeChat hook already returns isConnected - enhance the TopBar to show a connection status badge (green dot = connected, yellow = reconnecting, gray = disconnected).
Step 8: Add .env.local.example file in apps/quantchat/ with QUANTCHAT_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:3002 and NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL=ws://localhost:3002/ws.
acceptance_criteria: Sending a message triggers both REST POST and WS publish
Typing indicator shows when setTyping is called on input keypress
Read receipts are sent when messages are viewed
Connection status indicator is visible in the chat header
TypeScript compiles without errors: pnpm turbo typecheck --filter=@quant/quantchat
